2606.07069.title: mmPISA-bench: Do LLMs Reason Equally Well Across 43 Languages?
2606.07069.paper_summary: We introduce mmPISA-bench, a compact high-quality multilingual reasoning benchmark derived from the OECD Programme for International Student Assessment (PISA). The benchmark consists of 25 multiple-choice questions that require reasoning in order to be answered correctly. Each question is provided in official human translations to 43 languages and complemented with machine-translated counterparts (i.e., 2,150 data points in total). We evaluate two mainstream proprietary LLMs across languages, reasoning effort levels, and translation types in terms of their ability to answer the questions correctly. Our results show that modern LLMs can reason effectively across all evaluated languages, achieve accuracy comparable to human test-takers, with some performance variations across covered languages. We further find that machine-translated questions do not degrade accuracy relative to official human translations which suggests that high-quality machine translation (synthetic data) might often be adequate for large-scale multilingual reasoning evaluations where official translations are not available. Finally, we analyze token usage and related inference cost and find that LLMs usage in some languages is simultaneously more expensive and less accurate.
2606.07069.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 mmPISA-bench，一個源自經濟合作與發展組織國際學生評估計畫（PISA）的緊湊高品質多語言推理基準。<lb/>該基準由 25 道多選題組成，這些問題需要推理才能正確回答。<lb/>每個問題都提供了官方的人類翻譯，涵蓋 43 種語言，並附有機器翻譯的對應版本（即總共 2,150 個數據點）。<lb/>我們評估了兩個主流的專有大型語言模型（LLM），針對不同語言、推理努力程度和翻譯類型，檢視它們正確回答問題的能力。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，現代 LLM 能夠在所有評估的語言中有效推理，達到與人類考生相當的準確度，並在涵蓋的語言中存在一些性能變異。<lb/>我們進一步發現，機器翻譯的問題相較於官方的人類翻譯並未降低準確度，這表明高品質的機器翻譯（合成數據）在官方翻譯不可用的情況下，可能足以用於大規模的多語言推理評估。<lb/>最後，我們分析了標記使用情況和相關的推理成本，發現某些語言中 LLM 的使用同時更昂貴且準確度較低。
2606.07069.author: Yerzhan Sapenov et.al.
